prompt: The LRU cache is leaking memory. Items should be evicted when the cache reaches maxSize but the size keeps growing. Also, concurrent access panics with 'concurrent map writes'.

turns.content:
<task>
The LRU cache is leaking memory. Items should be evicted when the cache reaches maxSize but the size keeps growing. Also, concurrent access panics with 'concurrent map writes'.
</task>

arguments.reasoning:
Two bugs:

1. Memory leak in removeOldest() (line 47-52): It removes the element from the linked list (c.order.Remove) but NEVER deletes the key from the map (c.items). So the map grows forever even though the list is bounded. Fix: extract the key from the entry and delete it from c.items.

2. No mutex protection — all map/list operations are unprotected. Concurrent Get/Put will panic with 'concurrent map writes'. Fix: add sync.RWMutex.

Let me fix both.

arguments.summary:
Read pkg/cache/lru.go (56 lines). Found both bugs:
1. Memory leak at removeOldest() (line 47-52): removes element from linked list but never deletes key from c.items map. Map grows unbounded.
2. No mutex — all map/list operations are unprotected, causing 'concurrent map writes' panic.
Fixes: delete from map in removeOldest(), add sync.RWMutex to all methods.

turns.content:

ok  	github.com/user/cachesrv/pkg/cache	0.015s

arguments.summary: All 3 tests pass with -race flag (no data race detected). TestEviction confirms eviction works and Len()==2. TestConcurrentAccess runs 50 goroutines with no panic.
arguments.final_answer:
Fixed both LRU cache bugs in pkg/cache/lru.go:

1. Memory leak: removeOldest() removed elements from the linked list but never deleted keys from the items map. Added `delete(c.items, oldest.Value.(*entry).key)` to clean up the map entry.

2. Concurrent map writes: Added sync.RWMutex — Lock() for Get (needs write for MoveToFront) and Put, RLock() for Len. Get uses full Lock because MoveToFront mutates the list.

All 3 tests pass with -race flag.
